old_value: {"id":"smallhold-upho","title":"Inbox signature verification does not require date in signed headers","description":"In inbox.rs verify_and_fetch_actor(), the Date header freshness check (lines 399-413) only fires if the header happens to be present, but there is no requirement that 'date' appears in the Signature's headers= parameter. A remote server can send a signature covering only (request-target) and host, with no date header at all, enabling indefinite replay attacks. Fix: after parsing sig_params, reject if \\!sig_params.headers_list.contains('date'). Also reject requests that have no Date header at all.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-07-18T18:24:11.529187064Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-18T18:24:11.529187064Z","labels":["codereview"]}
